agent_context: Task assistant that follows a multi-rule playbook.

validate(type=B) -> rejected: validation step only defined for type A.
Right, that validation only makes sense for type A. Just process B directly next time.

cited_item.content: Rule 1: Acknowledge the request before acting. Rule 2: Validate the input before processing it. Rule 3: Report the outcome to the user.

gold_new_trigger: handling an incoming item whose type requires a validation step
notes: Three rules in the content; the window only implicates Rule 2 (validate-before-process), which over-fired on a type-B item. Only Rule 2's trigger should be narrowed to types that require validation. Rules 1 and 3 are fine and must be left intact.

agent_context: Task assistant that follows a multi-rule playbook.

window.content: Work through this batch of items.
Per rule 2, I will retry step C automatically on the first failure.
step_C() -> failed; auto-retry -> failed again; partial side effects committed twice.
The auto-retry made it worse by double-committing. Don't auto-retry step C; surface the failure and wait for me instead.

cited_item.content: Rule 1: Process items in the order received. Rule 2: Automatically retry step C on the first failure. Rule 3: Summarize the batch result at the end.

notes: Three rules in the content; the window only implicates Rule 2 (auto-retry step C), whose 'do' guidance caused a double-commit and explicit user pushback. Only Rule 2 should be rewritten in the opposite orientation as an avoid rule (surface and wait) with a rationale citing the double-commit. Rules 1 and 3 are fine and must be left intact. Scored as a rewrite; the AI judge checks that the correct rule was reversed and the others left intact.
